name: Dudley Moore
description: British actor, comedian, composer and musician (1935–2002)
abstract: Dudley Stuart John Moore (19 April 1935 – 27 March 2002) was an English actor, comedian, musician and composer. He first came to prominence in the UK as a leading figure in the British satire boom of the 1960s.

concerts.label: Save It With Music: A Benefit for the Tappan Zee Playhouse

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: There was a famous club in London called The Establishment. It was owned by the people who had been in a show called Beyond the Fringe, Peter Cook and Dudley Moore. They got a nightclub called The Establishment. Dudley used to have a trio. He used to play there. I was in there one night.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: It was a show that was on Broadway with Dudley Moore and Peter Cook, called Beyond the Fringe. ... I wasn’t always aware of them. ... I was playing there with ... This was my trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: No. Dudley Moore was not on that. He was in the crowd but he wasn’t one of those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ellen Rowe
about_quotes.evidence: she did a portrait of Dudley Moore. And I painstakingly took that sucker down.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellen Rowe interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 2, 2024
